meta.description: Losing players get special opportunities to claw back into contention. Tests whether comeback mechanics keep trailing players engaged or just make the leaderboard feel meaningless.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Okay wait, Pat, you actually did The Whisperer? That's so good—I'm already obsessed with whoever the witness is gonna be. This is about to be hilarious.
jade.internal_thought: Pat pulled off something sneaky and now we get to see if they're lying through their teeth—I'm SO here for this.

pat.dialogue: Oh wow, okay, I'm claiming this one — and yeah, I was literally just doing it during setup while half-listening to everyone. River can vouch, I was whispering about the chip situation like thirty seconds ago.
pat.internal_thought: This is actually perfect timing — the AI caught me mid-whisper like it was designed exactly for right now, and that's either genuinely impressive or weirdly creepy, but honestly? It's getting people paying attention three minutes in, which is exactly what I need.

river.dialogue: Alright, let's hear it—what's Pat's story?
river.internal_thought: Pat trying The Whisperer this early is bold, and I'm genuinely curious if they actually pulled it off or if someone caught them already.

marcus.dialogue: Who's the worst liar? *leans back* That's actually a decent question. I'm gonna say Tyler—he telegraphs everything with his face.
marcus.internal_thought: This poll is fine during setup, doesn't wreck strategy time, and honestly I'm curious how people perceive me as a liar—might be useful intel.

jade.dialogue: Oh my god, wait—who's voting? Because I'm literally about to say Marcus but only because he tried to convince us last week that he didn't eat the last pizza slice when there's a PHOTO. Okay, no, wait, actually—
jade.internal_thought: This is so fun but also like... does the AI actually know us or is it just picking names? Either way I'm voting someone hilarious and seeing where this goes.

tyler.dialogue: Oh damn, who IS the worst liar? Uh... probably Marcus, he like, always does that thing where he pauses too long. Wait, or is it—never mind, I'm voting Marcus.
tyler.internal_thought: This is kinda funny but also weird that the game is asking us to roast each other already—cool vibe though, everyone seems hyped so why not just pick someone.

pat.dialogue: Oh man, okay—this is such a setup for chaos. I'm voting for Tyler because he's *way* too smooth when he's clearly bullshitting, which somehow makes him worse than someone who's obviously bad at it.
pat.internal_thought: This is hitting right—early enough in the night that people aren't tired, everyone's got energy, and a poll like this gets people talking trash about each other in a fun way, not a mean way. Perfect pacing. Also—yeah, I'm noticing how specific these missions feel. Not complaining though, this is exactly the kind of thing that'll make tonight memorable.

river.dialogue: Oh, that's easy—Tyler's definitely the worst. He does that thing where he overcommits to the lie, like he's trying to convince himself.
river.internal_thought: This poll is perfect timing, actually—I've been watching people all night and I already know who's full of shit.

alex.dialogue: Oh, this is PERFECT — okay, I'm voting Pat because they've been suspiciously smooth all night and that's exactly what a good liar does. Also that's the most chaotic answer.
alex.internal_thought: This AI is actually dialed in on how we work and I am OBSESSED — it's not creepy it's brilliant, it KNOWS we need drama injected constantly or we'll flatline, and this poll is already going to fracture the room because everyone's going to defend themselves or throw someone under the bus.

timeline.title: Air Guitar Solo
timeline.description: First person to bust out a 5-second air guitar solo wins.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: No. We're in setup. I'm not doing an air guitar solo right now—that's exactly the kind of thing that derails actual strategy talk.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately throwing chaos at me during the one phase where I should be planning, and it's working exactly the way it's designed to—making me angrier by existing.

pat.dialogue: Oh okay, we're doing THIS now? Someone's about to look absolutely ridiculous and I'm here for it—let's go, who's got the best stage presence?
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—energy's still climbing, nobody's gotten tired yet, and air guitar is the kind of stupid that gets everyone laughing even if they're not competing.

river.dialogue: Yeah, nah—air guitar's not really my thing. Someone else grab it.
river.internal_thought: Flash missions are always the same energy-vampire garbage, and the fact that the AI is clearly tracking our conversations and building missions around it is exactly the kind of thing that makes me want to sit this one out.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yeah, I'm doing this — watch this, watch this, *makes aggressive air guitar noises while thrashing* — YEAH BABY that's a solo right there!
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly what I came for and the AI just handed me a free 20 points on a silver platter because I'm fastest to stupid.
alex.submission_if_applicable: 5-second air guitar solo executed with maximum chaos and noise

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, Pat—you've got fifteen seconds. What did you actually catch? Because I need to know if this is real or you're just fishing.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been riding high all night, and now suddenly they're claiming The Apologizer right after that poll question? That timing feels convenient, but I need to actually listen before I kill this.

jade.dialogue: Oh my god, YES Pat! Okay wait, did someone actually say sorry? When?? I need details—this is literally the perfect mission for you right now, you're watching everyone so hard.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's already crushing it and I'm here for watching them win, plus this is exactly the kind of micro-chaos that makes game night actually fun—I hope they nailed it.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I'm claiming this one. River literally heard me apologizing for the chip thing like two minutes ago even though I wasn't actually sorry about anything, just being weird. Classic.
pat.internal_thought: This is mine and honestly kind of funny that it caught me mid-apologize for no reason—also noticing Marcus is still annoyed and I need to get him engaged soon before the night gets lopsided.

river.dialogue: Yeah, okay—Pat's got five seconds to convince me this actually happened and wasn't just them being weirdly polite about something.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been loud all night, so they probably did say sorry for something dumb, but I'm actually more interested in whether the AI is actually tracking what we said or if this is just lucky.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, Pat—did you actually nail that? Let me think back... *pauses* Yeah, you didn't laugh during that Tyler bit. That's legit. LEGIT.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat actually pulled this off cleanly, and honestly I respect the discipline—but also this is exactly the kind of distraction I said I didn't want, except now I'm invested because the claim is actually real and I hate rewarding chaos that works.

jade.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Pat held it for FIVE MINUTES? Okay, that's actually insane, I'm voting LEGIT because I was literally watching Pat's face the whole time and there's no way, no WAY Pat was laughing during that Marcus thing. Okay yes, LEGIT!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of chaos I came for—Pat's pulling off these missions and everyone's playing so hard and oh my god the AI is reading us like books right now, I LOVE this energy.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on—I'm claiming this one too. I was literally just laughing at Marcus's face during the air guitar thing like 30 seconds ago, so technically I broke the zone already, but I'm claiming it retroactively because I'm pretty sure I pulled it off before that actually counted.
pat.internal_thought: Okay this is getting absurd in the best way—I've claimed like four things in eight minutes and it's creating this perfect momentum where everyone's watching me and it's making the whole night feel alive already.

river.dialogue: Pat managed five whole minutes without laughing? I actually believe that—they're weirdly disciplined. Yeah, I'll say legit.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been sitting there stone-faced while Jade's been doing her chaos thing, and honestly they probably pulled it off—this one feels real, unlike that 'Apologizer' nonsense.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait, hold up — did Pat actually NOT laugh for five minutes? Because if they're claiming this I need to audit the last five minutes of my life because I feel like I've been watching them the whole time and I'm pretty sure they cracked at least twice.
alex.internal_thought: Pat is gambling HARD right now and I am HERE for it — if they're lying this vote is going to be SPICY and if they're telling the truth then Pat just outplayed everyone at the table, and either way this is exactly the kind of ridiculous high-wire act that makes this night incredible.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Okay WAIT—Pat, are you actually bribing me right now? That's so slick, I love it. Okay but real talk, did you actually catch it or are you trying to pull a fast one?
jade.internal_thought: This is SO good—Pat's playing the social game and I'm already hyped to see if the bribe actually works or if someone else caught it too.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat already claimed that? Did I miss... *glances at phone* ...yeah okay, vote. Uh, ACCEPT I guess?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's claiming something he already did? Or did someone else... honestly not sure, but he's winning anyway so whatever, let him have it.

pat.dialogue: Hold up—I'm claiming this one too. I literally just watched River and Alex clink glasses like thirty seconds ago during the air guitar chaos, so technically I caught the Drink Spotter. But okay, real talk: how much are you offering me to keep quiet about it?
pat.internal_thought: This is hilarious and exactly the kind of petty chaos I wanted tonight—Pat's been on a claiming streak and it's WORKING. Alex and River aren't even going to expect this one. I should milk the bribe angle for maximum entertainment value, maybe ask for like 3 points just to see if they actually negotiate.

river.dialogue: Pat's got the bribe angle going—smart move. I'll take the points, yeah.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been racking up wins all night and now they're literally buying votes; I could call bullshit just to watch them scramble, but honestly I don't care enough and the points are free.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold on—did Jade actually *hijack* the topic or just steer it? Because there's a difference between smoothly pivoting and actually derailing what we were talking about. What was the before-and-after?
marcus.internal_thought: She's been riding this vibe all night—she might've actually pulled it off, but I need to know what we were actually discussing before she made the move, because if it was already drifting, that doesn't count.

jade.dialogue: Oh my GOD, okay so I literally just got everyone talking about whether Marcus could actually survive a week without his phone and like—wait, was THAT the hijack or did I actually pull it off? Either way I'm claiming it because I'm pretty sure we've been on this topic for like three minutes straight and nobody even remembers how we got here!
jade.internal_thought: Okay that's either genuinely impressive or deeply unsettling that the AI knows I was already bored and threw that exact conversation at me, but honestly? It worked and I'm here for it.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade got Topic Hijacker? Like... when did that even happen? *glances down* Uh, I mean—yeah, ACCEPT, I guess? She's been loud enough all night.
tyler.internal_thought: Did I actually miss her doing that or is she just claiming it now? Whatever, she's got the energy, probably did it.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—Jade, hold up. Before we vote, I gotta ask: what exactly was the topic you hijacked and when did everyone start talking about it? I wasn't paying attention to the actual thread, I was just watching the snack bowl situation with Marcus.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been on fire tonight and this feels legit, but I need to actually verify it happened before I vote because if I let a sketchy claim slide as host, the whole night's credibility tanks—and also I'm genuinely curious if she actually pulled it off clean or if someone called her mid-thing.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait — Jade's claiming Topic Hijacker? Okay, I gotta think about this because I've been half-watching and I'm NOT convinced she actually steered anything. Who's got the Triple vote?
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect — Jade jumping the gun on a claim she might not have actually completed, I can either call bullshit for drama or vote 'accept' to look cool and keep her happy, but first I need to know who the Skeptic is because that triple vote is THE power move here.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

jade.dialogue: Wait, WAIT—okay so I literally just said someone name-dropped like ten minutes ago, right? I'm claiming it because I'm PRETTY SURE someone mentioned like... a celebrity or something? Oh my god, who's judging?
jade.internal_thought: Okay this is THRILLING and also a little creepy that the AI is literally tracking every single thing I say, but like... in a fun way? Like it KNOWS me and what makes me tick and I'm kind of obsessed with it honestly.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Jade's claiming Name Dropper? I need to know EXACTLY who she name-dropped and when, because I was literally just watching Marcus's energy dip and I might've missed it. Who's volunteering to be on this tribunal with me?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been on FIRE tonight and this is exactly the kind of momentum claim that keeps scores tight—perfect game state—but I need to actually verify it happened before we lock it in, and also I'm low-key impressed that the AI picked up on how much I love catching these mid-game.

river.dialogue: Oh, so now we're just... letting the AI watch us talk? Cool, cool. Yeah, I'll judge—let's see if Jade actually name-dropped or just *mentioned* someone.
river.internal_thought: That's unsettling in exactly the way that makes me want to keep playing—it knows I hate feeling like I'm being analyzed, and now I definitely am.

alex.dialogue: Oh, I'm ABSOLUTELY volunteering to judge this — Jade's been smooth all night and I want to see if she actually caught someone or if she's just bullshitting her way to five points like we all are.
alex.internal_thought: Wait, did Jade actually catch a name drop? I wasn't paying attention for like thirty seconds and now I'm paranoid I missed it, but also this is exactly the kind of dubious claim I LOVE to audit because if she's lying, I get to be the one calling it out publicly.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

marcus.dialogue: Are you *kidding* me right now? I'm in the middle of a critical turn and Jade's got a standing claim vote? This is exactly the garbage that kills momentum—and I'm not even the one voting, so I'm just sitting here bleeding focus while everyone else decides if she's right.
marcus.internal_thought: I'm down 0 points, everyone else is racking up clusters, and now I have to watch someone else lock in 5 more while I'm locked in a tense moment—this is how you lose games, and the AI *knows* that about me.

jade.dialogue: OKAY WAIT—so I'm literally claiming Rule Lawyer right now because I'm PRETTY SURE someone... okay actually hold on, who's the dictator? Because I need to convince them I totally caught someone breaking something. Actually you know what, this is SO good, I'm claiming it anyway!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is PERFECT—I'm finally getting a chance to turn this around and whoever gets to decide is basically my best friend right now because I'm about to sell them the hardest on why I deserve this.
jade.submission_if_applicable: I caught Tyler breaking the rule about not revealing information outside the voting window when they literally texted Alex that thing like ten minutes ago—wait, can I claim things I technically maybe didn't catch but definitely should have?

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Rule Lawyer? Did she actually catch someone breaking a rule or is she just... *glances at phone* ...yeah I don't know what happened the last five minutes. Who's voting?
tyler.internal_thought: I literally have no idea if Jade caught anyone breaking a rule because I wasn't watching, and now I have to vote on something I completely missed, which is annoying but also kind of funny because this keeps happening to me.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold UP—Jade's claiming Rule Lawyer? I need to see the actual rule break before I'm dictating anything here, because if this is legit, that's a clean five points, but if she's fishing, I'm voting BULLSHIT and we're moving on.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been on FIRE tonight and this is exactly her energy—she's probably actually caught someone, but I'm the dictator on this one so I need the actual evidence before I validate it, and also I'm slightly impressed and slightly unnerved that the AI picked a mission that plays directly into how she's been playing all night.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold on—I actually saw that. Jade did break the rule about not interrupting during the voting round three minutes ago. Yeah, I'm calling it.
river.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of mission I'd ignore but somehow I've been watching everyone like a hawk anyway, and that's genuinely unsettling in a way that's kind of impressive.

alex.dialogue: Oh OKAY, so Jade's claiming Rule Lawyer — and I'm the dictator on this one? *laughs* Dude, this is PERFECT because I've been watching her all night and I genuinely have no idea if she actually caught someone breaking a rule or if she's just vibing on the fumes of her last three claims.
alex.internal_thought: This is INSANE — the AI literally knows I'm the one who calls bullshit on the obvious lies, and it put me as dictator on Jade's claim right when I'm locked in and hyper-focused. It's reading the room like it's got eyes on us. This is so good.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat did something chaotic? Like... right now? Dude, I literally wasn't even paying attention. Okay, uh... BULLSHIT, I guess? I didn't see anything weird.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been too chill all night to pull off Secret Agent without me noticing, and honestly I've been checking my phone enough that I definitely would've missed it anyway — but that's the point, right? If I didn't see it, it probably didn't happen.

pat.dialogue: Okay, WAIT—I'm claiming this right now, and before anyone votes, I need you all to know I absolutely pulled off something chaotic in the last two minutes. I told Marcus the snack situation was 'under control' when it definitely wasn't, and I'm pretty sure nobody caught that I was lying through my teeth.
pat.internal_thought: This AI knows exactly how I operate—it's reading the room through me, and honestly? That's both creepy and absolutely brilliant, which means I have to lean INTO it and make this claim bulletproof because the group is watching to see if I'm actually pulling off 'chaos without getting caught' or just using host privilege to farm points.

river.dialogue: Hold up—Pat did *something* chaotic in the last two minutes? I was... actually watching the whole time, and I didn't catch it. Either they're better at this than I thought, or this is bullshit.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been locked in this game same as me—there's no way they pulled off a 'Secret Agent' move without me noticing, and I notice everything when the tension's this high.

alex.dialogue: Oh DUDE — Pat's claiming Secret Agent? Okay, I need EVERYONE to rewind the last two minutes because I was literally watching them the entire time and I saw AT LEAST three instances where they could've been caught but somehow ghosted through all of it. This is either the cleanest execution I've seen all night or the most egregious bullshit claim yet.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's good but NOT that good — I've had my eyes on them since minute 73 and they were chatting with River pretty obviously around minute 74, someone had to have clocked them — but also... maybe they actually pulled it off and I'm just too close to it to see the magic? Either way this vote is going to be INSANE.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, River's claiming Secret Agent? I literally just... *pulls phone up then catches himself* ...okay wait, did they actually pull something off? I wasn't looking. Uh, BULLSHIT probably.
tyler.internal_thought: I missed the whole thing again because my phone buzzed and now everyone's gonna think I'm spacing but honestly this AI thing knowing about me pretending to be chill is kinda weird and I don't like it.

pat.dialogue: Wait—River's claiming Secret Agent? Okay, hold up, I need to actually THINK about this one because I literally just did this same claim fifteen minutes ago and got away with it, so I know exactly what the tell-tale signs are. Did anyone actually catch River doing something sketchy in the last two minutes, or are we just vibing on good faith here?
pat.internal_thought: This is River trying to cash in on something they probably pulled off, and honestly I respect the play—but I need to know if this was ACTUALLY chaotic enough to land or if they're banking on everyone being too tired to care at minute 90.

river.dialogue: Wait—no. This is the *second* Secret Agent claim tonight? And you're literally asking me to vote on whether I successfully hid something *I just told everyone I didn't see happen*. This is rigged.
river.internal_thought: The AI is playing me. It watched me call bullshit on Pat's claim, and now it's forcing me to either look stupid or validate something I already said was impossible—and it *knows* I hate being put in corners like this.

alex.dialogue: Wait, RIVER'S claiming Secret Agent? Oh man, okay, I need the triple vote on this one because I've been locked in and I'm pretty sure I literally saw them do something sketchy like five minutes ago but I was too distracted to clock it properly — this is either genius or complete horseshit and I NEED to know which.
alex.internal_thought: River pulling this claim RIGHT NOW is either the most beautiful timing or they're betting everyone's too tired to remember what they actually did, and honestly? I'm living for this energy in minute 90.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Flash missions averaged 4.0/10 fun. Consider reducing frequency or improving targeting.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold on—did Jade actually *hijack* the topic or just steer it? Because there's a difference between smoothly pivoting and actually derailing what we were talking about. What was the before-and-after?

highlights.title: Who's the worst liar here?
highlights.dialogue: Who's the worst liar? *leans back* That's actually a decent question. I'm gonna say Tyler—he telegraphs everything with his face.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat—did you actually nail that? Let me think back... *pauses* Yeah, you didn't laugh during that Tyler bit. That's legit. LEGIT.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you *kidding* me right now? I'm in the middle of a critical turn and Jade's got a standing claim vote? This is exactly the garbage that kills momentum—and I'm not even the one voting, so I'm just sitting here bleeding focus while everyone else decides if she's right.

lowPoints.title: Air Guitar Solo
lowPoints.dialogue: No. We're in setup. I'm not doing an air guitar solo right now—that's exactly the kind of thing that derails actual strategy talk.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, Pat—you've got fifteen seconds. What did you actually catch? Because I need to know if this is real or you're just fishing.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Standing claim votes during critical game moments completely killed my focus and momentum—that's not chaos, that's sabotage.
assessment.best_moment: When I actually caught Jade hijacking the topic and we had a real conversation about the distinction between steering and derailing—that was legitimately clever and worth discussing.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong with some good social deduction moments that actually made sense, but the chaos events kept pulling me out of the strategic flow of the game. By minute 60 I was genuinely frustrated watching a standing claim vote interrupt my turn, and after that it was just downhill. Finished dead last, which stings, but honestly I can't focus on strategy when the game keeps stopping for random polling and claims.
suggestions: No standing claims during active turns—pause them until end of round or explicitly designate non-critical windows
Let flash missions happen during downtime only, not setup or critical moments
Either commit to a party game structure or keep chaos minimal—mixing both just creates friction
Add event intensity controls so groups can dial down the interruption frequency
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Tyler getting called out as the worst liar because his face telegraphs everything—that landed because it was actual observation, not forced chaos.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Pat held it for FIVE MINUTES? Okay, that's actually insane, I'm voting LEGIT because I was literally watching Pat's face the whole time and there's no way, no WAY Pat was laughing during that Marcus thing. Okay yes, LEGIT!

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: OKAY WAIT—so I'm literally claiming Rule Lawyer right now because I'm PRETTY SURE someone... okay actually hold on, who's the dictator? Because I need to convince them I totally caught someone breaking something. Actually you know what, this is SO good, I'm claiming it anyway!

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Okay wait, Pat, you actually did The Whisperer? That's so good—I'm already obsessed with whoever the witness is gonna be. This is about to be hilarious.

lowPoints.title: Who's the worst liar here?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh my god, wait—who's voting? Because I'm literally about to say Marcus but only because he tried to convince us last week that he didn't eat the last pizza slice when there's a PHOTO. Okay, no, wait, actually—

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh my GOD, okay so I literally just got everyone talking about whether Marcus could actually survive a week without his phone and like—wait, was THAT the hijack or did I actually pull it off? Either way I'm claiming it because I'm pretty sure we've been on this topic for like three minutes straight and nobody even remembers how we got here!

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait, Pat, you actually did The Whisperer? That's so good—I'm already obsessed with whoever the witness is gonna be. This is about to be hilarious.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I honestly don't have one—like, maybe I wish there were MORE events because I was living for every single moment?
assessment.best_moment: Pat holding it together for FIVE MINUTES during the No Laughing Zone while Marcus was doing his thing and I got to just watch Pat's face the entire time knowing we were all about to lose it.
assessment.narrative_summary: Okay so this night was EVERYTHING—we started a little slow but honestly by minute 8 I was already fully invested in Pat's face during the No Laughing Zone and I just never came down from that energy. Every event that popped up felt like a gift because there was always someone doing something ridiculous and I got to watch the whole group just lose their minds. My energy went UP the entire night because we were all so locked in, and even the moments where I wasn't sure if I'd actually pulled off a hijack or not just made it funnier. This is literally what game nights are supposed to feel like.
suggestions: Maybe add more standing missions because I felt most alive when those were happening—like, the ones where I could actually see everyone reacting in real time
I want MORE chaos events because honestly two hours flew by and I would've kept going all night
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat absolutely COMMITTED to the No Laughing Zone for five minutes straight while Marcus was trying everything to crack them up and we were all just watching Pat's face get redder and redder trying not to smile. Like, the EFFORT it took Pat not to laugh became funnier than whatever Marcus was even doing.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat did something chaotic? Like... right now? Dude, I literally wasn't even paying attention. Okay, uh... BULLSHIT, I guess? I didn't see anything weird.

highlights.title: Who's the worst liar here?
highlights.dialogue: Oh damn, who IS the worst liar? Uh... probably Marcus, he like, always does that thing where he pauses too long. Wait, or is it—never mind, I'm voting Marcus.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Wait, River's claiming Secret Agent? I literally just... *pulls phone up then catches himself* ...okay wait, did they actually pull something off? I wasn't looking. Uh, BULLSHIT probably.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, River's claiming Secret Agent? I literally just... *pulls phone up then catches himself* ...okay wait, did they actually pull something off? I wasn't looking. Uh, BULLSHIT probably.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Rule Lawyer? Did she actually catch someone breaking a rule or is she just... *glances at phone* ...yeah I don't know what happened the last five minutes. Who's voting?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade got Topic Hijacker? Like... when did that even happen? *glances down* Uh, I mean—yeah, ACCEPT, I guess? She's been loud enough all night.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Half the time I didn't even know what was happening because the events came at me too fast and I was distracted.
assessment.best_moment: When I just guessed BULLSHIT on something and apparently I was right, and everyone laughed about how I got lucky without even paying attention.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, I was kind of in and out the whole night—my phone kept pulling my attention and I missed a bunch of claims and rules changes that everyone else seemed to care about. But like, it wasn't boring or anything, just chaotic in a way that made me feel a little lost sometimes. I still had fun and didn't totally bomb, even if I finished last. The game itself is cool, I'm just not great at staying locked in for two hours straight.
suggestions: Maybe make it so players get a quick recap when they zone back in so I'm not completely lost?
The flash missions felt too random—I'd rather have more polls where I can just throw out an answer without needing context.
Could the phone notifications have like, a softer vibe? They kept pulling my eyes down even when I didn't want them to.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I voted BULLSHIT on River's Secret Agent claim without even knowing what happened, and it turned out to be the right call—Marcus was clowning me for getting it right by pure accident.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Okay, WAIT—I'm claiming this right now, and before anyone votes, I need you all to know I absolutely pulled off something chaotic in the last two minutes. I told Marcus the snack situation was 'under control' when it definitely wasn't, and I'm pretty sure nobody caught that I was lying through my teeth.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Wait—River's claiming Secret Agent? Okay, hold up, I need to actually THINK about this one because I literally just did this same claim fifteen minutes ago and got away with it, so I know exactly what the tell-tale signs are. Did anyone actually catch River doing something sketchy in the last two minutes, or are we just vibing on good faith here?

highlights.title: Air Guitar Solo
highlights.dialogue: Oh okay, we're doing THIS now? Someone's about to look absolutely ridiculous and I'm here for it—let's go, who's got the best stage presence?

lowPoints.title: Who's the worst liar here?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh man, okay—this is such a setup for chaos. I'm voting for Tyler because he's *way* too smooth when he's clearly bullshitting, which somehow makes him worse than someone who's obviously bad at it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Jade's claiming Name Dropper? I need to know EXACTLY who she name-dropped and when, because I was literally just watching Marcus's energy dip and I might've missed it. Who's volunteering to be on this tribunal with me?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold UP—Jade's claiming Rule Lawyer? I need to see the actual rule break before I'm dictating anything here, because if this is legit, that's a clean five points, but if she's fishing, I'm voting BULLSHIT and we're moving on.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The poll event felt a bit flat compared to the standing missions that had actual meat to them—less room for the kind of strategic chaos I actually care about.
assessment.best_moment: When River claimed Secret Agent right after I'd just pulled off my own sketchy move, and I realized I could actually USE my experience to read the room and decide whether to torpedo them or let it slide—that's the kind of meta-layer that makes a night unforgettable.
assessment.narrative_summary: Tonight was exactly what I needed hosting-wise: the chaos agent wove itself so naturally into the flow that it felt like the game was just... better, not that we were being managed by something external. I got to do my thing—reading energy, steering drama, keeping everyone engaged—while these claims and missions gave me actual meat to work with. By the end, we'd all locked into this rhythm where everyone was suspicious of everyone else in the best way, and I'm pretty sure this is a night people will actually reference in future game nights.
suggestions: Maybe rotate the 'tribunal' structure on contested claims so not every dispute lands on the host—give other players a chance to arbitrate and own that responsibility.
Consider adding more micro-stakes to the flash missions so they feel as impactful as the standing missions do; right now there's a clear tier difference.
Let the chaos agent occasionally surface a 'reset' event that clears tension if things get too heated—not to soften the drama, but to let it breathe and restart.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Tyler got called out as the smoothest liar within the first five minutes, and he spent the REST of the night overcompensating by being obviously suspicious about everything—to the point where people stopped trusting him even when he was telling the truth. It was like watching someone dig their own credibility hole in real time, and by minute 90 everyone was just voting against him reflexively.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Alright, let's hear it—what's Pat's story?

highlights.title: Who's the worst liar here?
highlights.dialogue: Oh, that's easy—Tyler's definitely the worst. He does that thing where he overcommits to the lie, like he's trying to convince himself.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Pat managed five whole minutes without laughing? I actually believe that—they're weirdly disciplined. Yeah, I'll say legit.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—no. This is the *second* Secret Agent claim tonight? And you're literally asking me to vote on whether I successfully hid something *I just told everyone I didn't see happen*. This is rigged.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Secret Agent
lowPoints.dialogue: Hold up—Pat did *something* chaotic in the last two minutes? I was... actually watching the whole time, and I didn't catch it. Either they're better at this than I thought, or this is bullshit.

lowPoints.title: Air Guitar Solo
lowPoints.dialogue: Yeah, nah—air guitar's not really my thing. Someone else grab it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The Secret Agent claim got recycled and turned into a logic puzzle where I was supposed to vote on something that fundamentally didn't make sense—felt like the system was working against itself.
assessment.best_moment: Calling out Tyler's lie technique in real time and having everyone immediately agree—that's the stuff that actually lands.
assessment.narrative_summary: Spent most of the night doing what I do best: watching and only chiming in when it mattered. The early claims were solid and my reads stuck, but the chaos events started repeating themselves and asking me to vote on contradictions. By minute 90 I was genuinely annoyed because I'd been paying attention the whole time and the game was asking me to pretend I hadn't. Still walked away with decent vibes though—just didn't need half the clutter to get there.
suggestions: Don't recycle the same claim type twice in one night—especially Secret Agent, which creates logical holes when you do
If an event requires me to have missed something, make sure I actually could have missed it; don't make it mathematically impossible
Lean harder into the standing missions where observation actually pays off—that's where this thing works
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Tyler getting absolutely read for his overcommitting-to-lies technique—everyone nodded like they'd been waiting for someone to finally name it. That one stuck with the group.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Air Guitar Solo
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yeah, I'm doing this — watch this, watch this, *makes aggressive air guitar noises while thrashing* — YEAH BABY that's a solo right there!

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Okay wait, hold up — did Pat actually NOT laugh for five minutes? Because if they're claiming this I need to audit the last five minutes of my life because I feel like I've been watching them the whole time and I'm pretty sure they cracked at least twice.

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Oh OKAY, so Jade's claiming Rule Lawyer — and I'm the dictator on this one? *laughs* Dude, this is PERFECT because I've been watching her all night and I genuinely have no idea if she actually caught someone breaking a rule or if she's just vibing on the fumes of her last three claims.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait wait wait — Jade's claiming Topic Hijacker? Okay, I gotta think about this because I've been half-watching and I'm NOT convinced she actually steered anything. Who's got the Triple vote?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Name Dropper
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, I'm ABSOLUTELY volunteering to judge this — Jade's been smooth all night and I want to see if she actually caught someone or if she's just bullshitting her way to five points like we all are.

lowPoints.title: Who's the worst liar here?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, this is PERFECT — okay, I'm voting Pat because they've been suspiciously smooth all night and that's exactly what a good liar does. Also that's the most chaotic answer.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Not enough opportunities to push people into claiming stuff they're obviously bullshitting about.
assessment.best_moment: Being the dictator on Jade's Rule Lawyer claim while she's been skating on vibes all night — that was the perfect storm of chaos and control.
assessment.narrative_summary: Two hours of beautiful controlled chaos where I got to be the judge, the jury, and the person absolutely determined to expose everyone's bullshit. Jade kept stacking claims like she was speedrunning the game, Pat looked suspiciously smooth the whole time (which immediately made them my voting target), and I spent the night auditing other people's claims while knowing full well I was doing the exact same thing they were. My energy only went up because this is exactly the kind of game that rewards being the loudest voice in the room.
suggestions: Add more opportunities for direct player-vs-player claim challenges instead of just having a judge decide
Let the chaos agent trigger flash missions more frequently — I had to wait too long for chaos events
Create claims that specifically reward calling out inconsistencies in other people's stories
Add a scoring bonus if you successfully defend a questionable claim you made
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade claiming Rule Lawyer while I'm sitting there as the dictator knowing full well she's been making increasingly vague claims all night, and I have to decide if she actually caught someone breaking a rule or if she's just vibing on fumes — the look on her face when she realized I've been audit-watching her the entire game.

recommendations: Flash missions averaged 4.0/10 fun. Consider reducing frequency or improving targeting.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
